name: RF-DETR
tagline: Real-time detection without the AGPL question
description: Roboflow's real-time transformer architecture for detection and instance segmentation, built on a DINOv2 backbone and reported state-of-the-art on COCO and RF100-VL. Six detection sizes from Nano to 2XLarge, optimized for fine-tuning on custom datasets. Accepted at ICLR 2026.
why: A genuinely permissive, high-accuracy real-time detector — the strong Apache-2.0 alternative to the AGPL-licensed YOLO ecosystem for commercial use, with edge-friendly latency (2-17ms on a T4).
audience: CV engineers, product teams needing commercial-friendly detectors

setup.requirements: Python 3.10+, pip install rfdetr; a T4-class GPU or better for real-time

license.weights: Apache-2.0 for base sizes; XL/2XL and rfdetr_plus under Roboflow's custom PML 1.0
license.notes: Stick to the base sizes for a clean Apache story; the largest variants carry a custom non-OSI license.

limitations: Segmentation is instance-level only, and published latency figures assume TensorRT with FP16.
take: The detector to reach for when legal asks 'what's our YOLO exposure?' and you'd rather not have that meeting.
